title: 10 best herbal teas for eczema
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eczema are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help alleviate eczema symptoms. These natural remedies offer an alternative to traditional medications and can improve the quality of life for those suffering from eczema. Some popular herbal teas known to help include chamomile, calendula, and nettle. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin, while calendula helps promote skin healing and nettle boasts antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects. By drinking these teas, individuals may experience reduced redness, itching, and inflammation, allowing them to enjoy daily activities without constant discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The tea's antioxidant compounds also soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections that can exacerbate eczema, promoting a healthier skin environment and reducing the risk of flare-ups. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to manage symptoms and promote healing in those affected by eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and redness associated with eczema by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial compounds in yarrow tea, including sesquiterpenes and lactones, help to prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms and promote healing.

Soften and moisturize skin properties: Yarrow tea's saponins and glycosides have been shown to gently soften and moisturize the skin, reducing itching and scaling associated with dry, itchy eczema patches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Combine 1 tablespoon of herb with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or carafe.
Drink the tea hot, or let it cool and refrigerate for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with eczema because it provides a soothing, calming effect on the skin. The marshmallow plant contains mucilage, a natural gel-like substance that coats and protects the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. As the tea is absorbed into the bloodstream, it also helps to moisturize and hydrate the skin from the inside out, promoting a healthy barrier against environmental stressors and irritants. This combination of soothing and moisturizing properties makes marshmallow teas a popular natural remedy for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated skin, providing relief from itching and inflammation associated with eczema.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by eczema.

Emollient and moisturizing effects: Marshmallow tea's mucilage content helps to lock in moisture, leaving skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated, reducing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, a thick, soothing substance that helps to calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation associated with eczema.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, possess anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to alleviate symptoms of eczema, such as redness and itching.
remedy_recipe: Measure two tablespoons of dried marshmallow root into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat one cup of filtered water in a kettle or on the stove until it reaches a simmer temperature.
Pour the hot water over the dried marshmallow root and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth to catch the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, and enjoy within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the burdock plant contains lignans, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin and alleviate symptoms of eczema such as itching and redness. Burdock tea also has antioxidant and soothing effects that help calm irritated skin, promoting a reduction in eczema lesions and improving overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, providing relief to irritated skin.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in burdock tea help combat free radicals that can damage the skin and exacerbate eczema symptoms, promoting overall skin health.
Anti-allergic: Burdock tea's anti-allergic properties help reduce the body's allergic response, which can contribute to eczema outbreaks by alleviating inflammation and itching caused by allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm irritated skin.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried burdock roots or fresh burdock leaves and flowers from trusted sources.
Measure one teaspoon of dried burdock root or two tablespoons of fresh leaves and flowers per cup of boiling water.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan and bring to a boil then reduce heat to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug or teapot.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 2-5 minutes before serving hot or cold with honey and lemon to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm itchy skin. The tea's antioxidants also help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing redness. Additionally, pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, further alleviating eczema symptoms. Drinking this tea regularly can provide long-term relief from the discomfort and embarrassment of eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common characteristic of eczema, and can help soothe itchy and irritated skin.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms such as dryness and itching.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold tea has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and can help accelerate the recovery process of eczema lesions, reducing the risk of infection and scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated skin associated with eczema.
Seeds: High in anti-inflammatory properties, seeds work to reduce redness and swelling caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers, ensuring they are dry and free of contaminants.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers or three teaspoons of fresh petals for every cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured calendula with 200ml of boiling water in a medium-sized pot and steep for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large ceramic teapot, discarding solids.
Serve the herbal tea hot or chilled, garnished with lemon slices if desired, within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with eczema because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The high concentration of flavonoids, amino acids, and catechins in white tea help to soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating eczema symptoms. Additionally, the caffeine content in white tea can also help to calm down irritated skin and promote a sense of relaxation, further enhancing its benefits for eczema sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal white tea contains high levels of antioxidants like polyphenols, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, alleviating symptoms of eczema.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbs like green tea, chamomile, and lavender present in herbal white teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe itchy skin, reduce redness, and prevent further irritation.

Calming flavonoids: Flavonoids like epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) in green tea can help calm the immune system's overactive response that contributes to eczema, reducing the risk of flare-ups and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for soothing and calming itchy skin associated with eczema, as they contain anti-inflammatory compounds.
Bark: The bark is often used to treat skin conditions like eczema due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves at peak freshness for optimal flavor quality.
Rinse 10 grams of fresh white tea leaves with filtered water to remove impurities.
Steep the rinsed tea leaves in 250ml boiling water for exactly 2-3 minutes.
Strain the tea liquid through a fine-mesh sieve to remove loose leaves and solids.
Cool the tea to room temperature or chill it in the refrigerator for serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with eczema because its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties can provide relief from itchy, irritated skin. The apigenin present in chamomile tea has been shown to reduce inflammation and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema. Additionally, chamomile's natural antibacterial and antifungal agents help to prevent infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, promoting a healthy and balanced skin environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: German Chamomile tea contains flavonoids, terpenes, and other anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin, providing relief from eczema symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in German Chamomile tea help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema and lead to chronic inflammation.

Calming effects on skin and nervous system: The apigenin and other active compounds in German Chamomile tea have a calming effect on the skin and nervous system, reducing anxiety, stress, and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create teas that soothe and calm skin irritations associated with eczema.
Flowers: Delicate and fragrant, flowers are prized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried german chamomile flowers from your garden or store-bought packaging containing one teaspoon of dried flowers per tea bag.
Boil filtered water in a teapot or kettle and pour it over the german chamomile tea bags or loose flowers in a teapot for three to five minutes.
Steep the herbal mixture in hot water at 195°f to 205°f for five to seven minutes allowing the chamomile's volatile oils to infuse into the liquid.
Remove the tea bag or strain the tea leaves from the pot and pour the soothing yellowish-green tea into a cup for immediate consumption.
Serve the herbal german chamomile tea warm or at room temperature with or without honey and lemon to taste after preparation in approximately seven minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory teas helps with eczema because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in chicory roots have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to eczema symptoms. Additionally, chicory tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate eczema. This natural remedy may provide relief from the discomfort and itching associated with eczema, promoting overall skin health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Chicory tea contains antioxidants like chlorogenic acid, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, thereby alleviating eczema symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The polyphenolic compounds present in chicory tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema.

Moisturizing properties: Chicory tea has been shown to enhance the production of ceramides, which are important lipids that help to maintain the skin's natural barrier function, thus providing relief from dry, itchy skin characteristic of eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chicory have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin caused by eczema.
Rhyzomes: Chicory rhyzomes contain saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried chicory root for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat the water in a medium saucepan until it reaches a rolling boil.
Steep the dried chicory root in the boiling water for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with eczema because it is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and silicates that soothe and calm irritated skin. The antioxidants combat inflammation, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. Silicates, in particular, help to repair and strengthen the skin's natural barrier function, allowing it to better retain moisture and protect against environmental irritants. Additionally, horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of compound found in horsetail tea, have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (relieving itching) effects, making them effective in reducing the inflammation and discomfort associated with eczema.

Silica: Horsetail tea is rich in silica, which has been shown to improve skin elasticity and firmness, helping to reduce the appearance of skin dryness and irritation often experienced by those with eczema.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in horsetail tea help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and skin irritation, exacerbating eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica and other minerals, horsetail roots help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation associated with eczema.
Bark: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, horsetail bark helps to reduce redness and swelling caused by eczema, promoting a more comfortable healing process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality horsetail herb equisetum arvense fresh or dried leaves.
Rinse and chop the harvested horsetail leaves to release their flavors and oils.
Combine horsetail leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to meld.
Strain and serve the herbal tea hot or cooled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which soothe and calm itchy, irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and essential oils work to reduce inflammation, ease itching, and promote healing. Additionally, fennel's natural antibacterial properties help combat infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. By drinking this tea regularly, individuals with eczema may experience reduced redness, swelling, and discomfort, ultimately improving their overall skin health and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which can reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema, providing relief to affected skin.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants in fennel tea can help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, such as redness, swelling, and irritation, promoting overall skin health.

Soothing demulcent properties: Fennel tea's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming itchy, dry, and irritated skin, providing relief from eczema symptoms like itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy and inflamed skin associated with eczema.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of eczema such as itching and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried or fresh fennel leaves from foeniculum vulgare plants in abundance.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried fennel leaves or 3-4 sprigs of fresh fennel per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the measured fennel ingredients in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release essential oils and flavors.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking or add honey to taste as desired for sweetness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with eczema because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The flavonoids and polyphenols present in licorice root have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema. Additionally, the tea's antiseptic properties help prevent infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin, herbal licorice teas can provide relief and promote healing for individuals suffering from eczema.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in licorice tea have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin is a compound found in licorice that has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, which can contribute to eczema symptoms.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in licorice tea help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to inflammation, making it a helpful adjunct treatment for eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant is rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it an excellent addition to medicinal teas for eczema.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root.
Bring one cup of water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan.
Add the measured root and simmer for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional three to five minutes before serving.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eczema with herbal teas is by regularly consuming chamomile or calendula tea, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin. Additionally, drinking nettle tea can provide relief from eczema symptoms due to its high mineral and nutrient content. Always consult with a healthcare professional before incorporating herbal teas into your treatment plan.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eczema that are treated with herbal teas include psoriasis and dermatitis. Chamomile tea is often used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe irritated skin. Green tea is rich in polyphenols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin hydration. Nettle tea is also known to alleviate symptoms of skin conditions due to its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedies.
